introSection: The Flash: Vertical Velocity, previously known as Vertical Velocity or V2, is a steel inverted launched shuttle roller coaster located at Six Flags Great America in Gurnee, Illinois, USA. Opened on May 18, 2001, the ride is located in the Yankee Harbor section of the park. It is an Impulse Coaster from Intamin.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
The addition of Vertical Velocity was announced in early 2001.

Vertical Velocity opened on May 18, 2001.

In 2004, additional supports were added to both towers to increase stability. In September 2021, Vertical Velocity was closed for the remainder of the year. It was subsequently repainted with red supports.

On March 24, 2022, Six Flags Great America announced that Vertical Velocity would be rethemed to The Flash: Vertical Velocity as part of the new DC Universe area.

Since the ride's renaming, its V2 sign has been used as a prop in the Six Saint's Cemetery, a graveyard paying tribute to the park's past attractions during the park's Fright Fest event.

sections.title: Trains
sections.content: Single train with 7 cars. Riders are arranged 2 across in 2 rows for a total of 28 riders. Riders are held in by over-the-shoulder harnesses.

sections.title: Color scheme
sections.content: Yellow track and red supports. Prior to its repaint in the fall of 2021, the supports were blue.

fullText:
The Flash: Vertical Velocity, previously known as Vertical Velocity or V2, is a steel inverted launched shuttle roller coaster located at Six Flags Great America in Gurnee, Illinois, USA. Opened on May 18, 2001, the ride is located in the Yankee Harbor section of the park. It is an Impulse Coaster from Intamin.

The addition of Vertical Velocity was announced in early 2001.

Vertical Velocity opened on May 18, 2001.

In 2004, additional supports were added to both towers to increase stability. In September 2021, Vertical Velocity was closed for the remainder of the year. It was subsequently repainted with red supports.

On March 24, 2022, Six Flags Great America announced that Vertical Velocity would be rethemed to The Flash: Vertical Velocity as part of the new DC Universe area.

Since the ride's renaming, its V2 sign has been used as a prop in the Six Saint's Cemetery, a graveyard paying tribute to the park's past attractions during the park's Fright Fest event.



Single train with 7 cars. Riders are arranged 2 across in 2 rows for a total of 28 riders. Riders are held in by over-the-shoulder harnesses.

Yellow track and red supports. Prior to its repaint in the fall of 2021, the supports were blue.
